reasoning: The baseline value of 1.000 for Lesotho over the last 360 days means there have been zero (or nearly zero) fatalities recorded by ACLED in this period. For the probability to trigger, Lesotho would need over 10 fatalities in the rolling 30-day period prior to each resolution date. Lesotho is historically relatively peaceful in terms of organized political violence or armed conflict compared to some other regions, although there have been instances of political instability and occasional police brutality. Based on extensive searches there are no credible reports of significant violent events, mass protests, or political crises occurring in Lesotho in January or February 2026. A sudden spike in violence leading to >10 fatalities in a 30-day span is highly unlikely without an observable precipitating event. The uncertainty increases slightly over the forecast horizon, justifying the bump from 0.05 on Feb 22, 2026, to 0.06 on Mar 17, 2026.
